channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Brooklyn Co-op Divided Over Push to Boycott Israeli Products
item.description: The Park Slope Food Co-op, a socially conscious grocery store in a liberal neighborhood, has devolved into dysfunction ahead of a Tuesday vote on the proposed boycott.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy and Liam Stack
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Brandeis, Louis D, Center for Human Rights Under Law

media:content.media:credit: George Etheredge for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Some members of the Park Slope Food Co-op want the grocery store to stop selling about a dozen Israeli products as a response to the crisis in the Middle East.

item.title: Everyone Knew Them on the Block. Then a Friday Ritual Ended in Tragedy.
item.description: The group of friends had used the same corner on the Upper West Side to unwind for years. By the end of the night, two people would be dead after a speeding S.U.V. drove into a crowd.
item.dc:creator: Nate Schweber

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A memorial for Jason Negron and Michael Saint-Hilaire, two friends who were killed when a car drove onto a sidewalk in Morningside Heights.

item.title: Lawmakers Face a Personal Fiscal Crisis: No Budget, No Paycheck
item.description: New York State’s budget is the most delayed it’s been since 2010. That means legislators have gone without pay, creating financial hardships familiar to many New Yorkers.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Bing Guan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Assemblywoman Emily Gallagher said she has had to borrow money from family to help pay her $2,750 monthly rent in Brooklyn during the legislative pay freeze.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 25, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: ‘Star City,’ and 5 More Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: A spinoff of “For All Mankind” airs on Apple TV, and a new Netflix series tells the incredible story of Brazil’s win at the 1970 World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:description: “Star City” expands the story lines of the series “For All Mankind,” this time from the perspective of the Soviet Union.

item.title: The Ghosts of France’s Slave Trade Will Be Silenced No More
item.description: Three centuries late and not a moment too soon.
item.dc:creator: Andrew S. Curran
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

item.title: My Wife Fought Corruption in El Salvador. Then She Vanished Into Its Prisons.
item.description: The cost of Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on crime is too high.
item.dc:creator: Louis Benavides
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

item.title: El papa León está a punto de hacer una declaración sobre la IA. Por qué es importante
item.description: León XIV va a emitir sus pensamientos sobre la inteligencia artificial mediante una forma de comunicación papal centenaria llamada encíclica, la primera desde que es papa.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo

media:content.media:description: El papa León ha hecho de la inteligencia artificial un tema central de su primer año como pontífice.

item.title: How Wine, Truffles and Honey Could Help Europe Fight Wildfires
item.description: Countries are learning that the finer things in life sometimes have a serendipitous side benefit.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek

media:content.media:credit: Samuel Aranda for The New York Times

item.title: Quote of the Day: Advice in a Bleak Job Market From Those Who Can Relate
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, May 25, 2026.

item.title: $140,000 E.V.s and Heritage Gold: The Rise of China’s Homegrown Luxury Market
item.description: As China’s economy slows, consumers are increasingly turning to domestic luxury products, challenging the dominance of long-established European brands.
item.dc:creator: Joy Dong

media:content.media:credit: Gilles Sabrié for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Li Maozai in his Maextro S800 sedan, built by Huawei and JAC Motors.

item.title: Pope Leo Is Set to Release an Encyclical About A.I. Why Is That Important?
item.description: Leo XIV is to issue his thoughts about artificial intelligence in the modern world, using a centuries-old form of papal communication called an encyclical, the first since he became pope.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo has made artificial intelligence a central theme of his first year as pontiff.

item.title: No Corrections: May 25, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, May 25, 2026.

item.title: Rubio Rebukes Hezbollah Chief Over Call for Lebanese to ‘Take to the Streets’
item.description: The militant leader, in a speech, had criticized the Lebanese government for engaging in direct talks with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:description: A building hit by an Israeli strike in Tyre, Lebanon, on Saturday.

item.title: A Democrat Took on Red Sox Ownership in an Ad. A Network Pulled it.
item.description: Graham Platner, a Senate candidate in Maine, blamed private equity for destroying “our favorite baseball team.” NESN, which is owned by the team, pulled his campaign ad, citing an intellectual property violation.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson and Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, a candidate for Senate in Maine, speaking at a town hall in Sabattus, Maine, last month.

item.title: In Ukraine, a Divisive 20th-Century Hero Comes Home
item.description: Andriy Melnyk, criticized as a Nazi collaborator and lionized as an anti-Soviet resistance leader, was given state honors for his reburial near Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer

media:content.media:credit: Andriy Zhyhaylo/Global Images Ukraine, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The coffins of Andriy Melnyk and his wife, Sofia Fedak-Melnyk, in Kyiv, Ukraine, on Friday.

item.title: Confusion and Worry After Trump Administration’s Abrupt Green Card Changes
item.description: Immigrants and their advocates and lawyers are trying to interpret a new Trump administration rule that requires people to be in their native country to apply for a green card.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales

media:content.media:description: The Citizenship and Immigration Services agency oversees the citizenship system.

item.title: Oil Prices Fall Sharply on News of Possible Iran Deal
item.description: S&P 500 futures were up slightly in what may be a sign that investors and analysts are waiting to see if a deal is finalized.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: What to Know About the Citizenship Lists Trump Wants to Create
item.description: President Trump is trying to create individual lists of citizens by state to determine who can vote, even as his administration acknowledges they would be unreliable.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella

media:content.media:description: President Trump signed an executive order in March that called on states to use federal databases to identify noncitizens.

item.title: Secretary Rubio Says U.S. Made “Significant Progress” Toward Interim Deal with Iran
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the Trump administration made “significant progress” toward a deal with Iran. A senior U.S. official also said that the U.S. and Iran had agreed in principle to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and to have Iran dispose of its nuclear material. Iran has not commented publicly on the potential agreement.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said nuclear talks with Iran may take place.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 25, 2026
item.description: Looking for answers to Anthony Grubb’s puzzle? You’re getting warmer.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: Reopening of Strait of Hormuz Still a Question Mark
item.description: Without the details of a formal agreement, it is unclear how soon normal shipping will resume and when oil prices will start to come down.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: About 1,500 to 2,000 ships have been trapped in the Persian Gulf by the conflict.

item.title: The Oscars Start at Cannes
item.description: Glamorous and a touch old-fashioned, the French film festival increasingly defines what Hollywood celebrates each year.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Israel’s President, Isaac Herzog, Denounces Israeli Violence
item.description: In a speech, Isaac Herzog, whose role is largely ceremonial, delivered a grave indictment of settler attacks in the West Bank and abuse of prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:description: President Isaac Herzog of Israel in Panama this month.

item.title: Caught Flat-Footed, a City Races to Catch Up With Ebola
item.description: The deadly virus has spread alarmingly in Congo for months. Only now is the response taking shape.
item.dc:creator: Declan Walsh and Arlette Bashizi

media:content.media:description: Disinfecting the public market on Saturday in Bunia, in the Democratic Republic of Congo’s northeast, as part of the response to the Ebola virus’s resurgence.

item.title: Manny Nosowsky, Waggish Crossword Constructor for The New York Times, Dies at 94
item.description: For nearly two decades, the prolific puzzle maker, a retired urologist, charmed solvers with his penchant for witty, waggish puns.
item.dc:creator: Deb Amlen

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by The New York Times. Photo, via Megalo Media, Inc.
media:content.media:description: Manny Nosowsky holds the record for the most puzzles published in The Times.

item.title: Senate Republicans Cast Doubt on a Potential Peace Deal With Iran
item.description: Lawmakers, including some of President Trump’s closest allies, slammed the emerging agreement as effectively undermining the president’s own war goals.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Senator Thom Tillis said it was “questionable” that Iran would end its blockade on the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Lo que hay que saber sobre el posible acuerdo de paz entre EE. UU. e Irán
item.description: El presidente Trump dijo que estaba cerca de alcanzar un acuerdo con Irán. Sin embargo, hasta el domingo por la mañana no estaba claro qué habían acordado EE. UU. e Irán.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gente caminando cerca de una valla publicitaria que representa el cierre del estrecho de Ormuz en Teherán, Irán, este mes.

item.title: Sobreviví al ébola. Esto es lo que más me asusta de este brote
item.description: El gobierno de Donald Trump recortó los programas destinados a coordinar una respuesta a esta enfermedad.
item.dc:creator: Craig Spencer
item.category: United States Agency for International Development

item.title: For Carlo Petrini, the Point of ‘Slow Food’ Wasn’t the Food. It Was Us.
item.description: The Italian activist, who died on Thursday, built his global movement on the idea that eating well could make modern life more meaningful.
item.dc:creator: Pete Wells

item.title: Gunman Shot and Killed by Secret Service Near White House
item.description: A gunman known to the U.S. Secret Service opened fire near the White House and was shot and killed by federal officers on Saturday evening.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:description: Gunfire broke out near 17th Street and Pennsylvania Avenue, at the northwestern corner of the White House grounds.

item.title: Bystander Wounded in White House Shooting Is in Stable Condition
item.description: The Metropolitan Police Department is investigating to determine who shot the bystander, who underwent surgery, and how many bullets were fired.
item.dc:creator: Katie Benner

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Law enforcement officers near the White House after a shooting on Saturday evening.

item.title: ‘Mandalorian and Grogu’ Tops Box Office as Disney Bets on ‘Star Wars’ Revival
item.description: The movie, which cost $300 million to make and market, was expected to collect about $102 million from Thursday through Monday at domestic theaters.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes
item.category: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu (Movie)

media:content.media:credit: Andres Kudacki for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu” was intended to sell toys as much as movie tickets.

item.title: Rafe Pomerance, the Paul Revere of Climate Change, Dies at 79
item.description: An environmental lobbyist and activist, he was a pivotal figure in drawing public attention and political support to the existential issue.
item.dc:creator: Trip Gabriel

media:content.media:description: Rafe Pomerance as president of the environmental group Friends of the Earth, in 1983. “He recruited us and energized us and gave us facts that allowed us to work on the most important issue of our time,” said Daniel Becker, a longtime leader with the Sierra Club.

item.title: Crack Forms in Garden Grove Chemical Tank, a Positive Sign for Firefighters
item.description: The tank in Southern California has been building in temperature and pressure for days, which could lead to an explosive release. The crack may reduce some of that pressure, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Dulany and Shawn Hubler

media:content.media:description: An industrial tank in Southern California is at risk of rupture or explosion that could send toxic chemicals into the surrounding area.

item.title: What to Know About the White House Shooting on Memorial Day Weekend
item.description: Shots were fired near the northwestern corner of the White House complex, while President Trump was inside the building. The Secret Service returned fire, killing the gunman.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson and Chelsia Rose Marcius

media:content.media:description: Shots were fired at the northwestern corner of the White House grounds.

item.title: Rubio Says Details on Iran Nuclear Program Still to Be Negotiated
item.description: The Secretary of State said the United States was prepared to begin those talks if Iran opens the Strait of Hormuz as part of an apparently emerging deal on the war.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said nuclear talks with Iran may take place.

item.title: These Are 5 of the Main Issues to Be Resolved in an Iran-U.S. Peace Deal
item.description: President Trump says the U.S. and Iran could be close to a peace deal. The big issues at stake include the fate of Iran’s nuclear program and reopening the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Members of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps at a government-organized demonstration in support of Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, the new supreme leader, in April.

item.title: To Get the Strait Open, Trump Had to Leave the Hardest Issues for Later
item.description: President Trump is hailing the agreement with Iran as groundbreaking, even as he admits it “isn’t even fully negotiated.” But the nuclear stockpile, enrichment and missiles have not been discussed.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

media:content.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz has been a focal point since the start of the conflict.

item.title: Pulled Pork at the Push of a Button
item.description: Assembling this hands-off slow cooker BBQ pulled pork this morning means you can revel in the complex, sweet-spicy dish tonight (and enjoy leftovers all week).
item.dc:creator: Melissa Clark

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Liebe for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.
media:content.media:description: Margaux Laskey’s slow cooker BBQ pulled pork.

item.title: Bonnaroo, Governors Ball and 7 More Summer Music Festivals Not to Miss
item.description: Can’t get to Lollapalooza or Outside Lands this year? Want to see Lizzo play flute with the Chicago Symphony Orchestra? Here are some options.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dowling

media:content.media:description: The Ferris wheel at the Bonnaroo Musical and Arts Festival in Manchester, Tenn., last year. The long-running festival will return in June, along with six stages featuring bands such as the Strokes and Geese.

item.title: Storms Expected Across Eastern U.S. Through Memorial Day
item.description: Rain and thunderstorms were forecast from the Gulf Coast northeastward through the Southeast and into parts of the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar

media:content.media:description: Placing flags at graves at Alexandria National Cemetery in Pineville, La. Wet weather was expected to dominate the eastern part of the United States through Memorial Day.

item.title: Memories of Growing Up on Long Island
item.description: A guest essayist wrote that Long Island produced “more than its share of villainous, dead-eyed jerks.” Readers offer their perspectives. Also: Honor at Stanford.

item.title: Grilled Salmon Salad With Lime, Chiles and Herbs
item.description: And four more bright, easy recipes for your post-holiday week.
item.dc:creator: Emily Weinstein

media:content.media:credit: David Malosh for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.

item.title: Rubio Says U.S.-India Ties Are Strong, Despite Fury Over Trump’s Actions
item.description: In New Delhi, Secretary of State Marco Rubio tried to explain why President Trump has pushed aggressive trade and immigration policies affecting India and Indians in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong

media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, at a news conference on Sunday in New Delhi, said the relationship between India and the United States “has not lost any momentum.”

item.title: Iran Projects Victory in Potential Deal With Washington
item.description: Iran’s leaders argue that the possible preliminary agreement has not extracted any major concessions.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Iranians waving flags last month at a government-organized demonstration in Tehran, the capital.

item.title: Fate of War in Lebanon Rests Mostly With Outside Powers
item.description: A U.S.-Iran peace deal could calm the conflict in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah. But that will depend mainly on what the United States, Iran and Israel decide.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A building hit by an Israeli strike, as seen through a broken window at the Hiram Hospital in Tyre, Lebanon, on Saturday.

item.title: Colbert’s Connection
item.description: A reflection on Stephen Colbert’s relationship with his audience.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wright-Piersanti
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: At Least 14 Killed in Bombing Attack That Derailed a Train in Pakistan
item.description: The explosion was so powerful that several nearby buildings were damaged and a resident 15 miles away was jolted awake by the blast. A separatist group claimed responsibility.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman

media:content.media:description: A Pakistani soldier aiming his weapon after an explosion struck a passenger train in Quetta, Pakistan, on Sunday.

item.title: Israeli Leader Says He and Trump Agree On Blocking an Iranian Nuclear Weapon
item.description: Analysts said Israel was worried that a potential agreement would not significantly degrade Iran’s nuclear and missile capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Marc Israel Sellem
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel at a ceremony on the eve of Israel’s day of remembrance for fallen soldiers in Jerusalem in April.
